description: Touch and temperature reach a body through 'hisae', its living outer organ. Skin also protects the tissue beneath it. 'leko' (surface) applies to the outside layer of an object, while 'kori' (leather) is processed animal material.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced air pass through the open glottis for 'h', then raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i'. Bring the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's', direct air through the narrow central channel, and open into stressed central 'a'. Move the tongue forward into middle-height unrounded 'e' for the separate final syllable.
usage_notes: Another body-part noun can locate the skin being discussed. Conditions on the skin take their own quality, event, or medical vocabulary.

semantic_domains.physical: the living outer organ of a human or animal body
